title.zh: 《Value Proposition Design / 价值主张设计》

summary.en: Strategyzer's practical guide for turning customer jobs, pains, and gains into value propositions, then testing whether the fit is real before scaling the business model.
summary.zh: Strategyzer 关于价值主张设计的实践指南：把客户任务、痛点和收益转化为价值主张，并在扩大投入前验证匹配是否真实。

recommendation.en: Use this when BMC's Customer Segments and Value Proposition are too generic. It gives teams a concrete way to describe customers, design fit, and connect discovery work to experiments.
recommendation.zh: 当 BMC 里的客户细分和价值主张过于泛泛时，推荐用这本。它能把客户描述、价值设计和实验验证连起来，避免价值主张停留在营销口号。

sources.label: Alexander Osterwalder, Yves Pigneur, Greg Bernarda & Alan Smith · Value Proposition Design: How to Create Products and Services Customers Want · Wiley · 2014 · ISBN 978-1118968055 · 320 pages
Local review · Value+Proposition+FULL.pdf and Value Proposition Design EPUB in BusinessBooks · complete book used for case extraction
Strategyzer · Value Proposition Design book page and free preview
Amazon · Value Proposition Design product page
Wiley · Value Proposition Design product page

headline.en: Turn customer understanding into a testable value proposition
headline.zh: 把客户理解转成可验证的价值主张

whyOpen.en: Open this when Customer Segments and Value Propositions in BMC are still generic. It gives you the customer profile, value map, and fit logic needed before experiments.
whyOpen.zh: 当 BMC 里的客户细分和价值主张还很泛时打开它：它提供客户档案、价值地图和匹配逻辑，让实验开始前先有清楚假设。

audience.en: Founders, product teams, discovery teams, and anyone translating customer interviews into product decisions.
audience.zh: 适合创业者、产品团队、客户发现团队，以及需要把访谈转成产品决策的人。

keyConcepts.zh: 规模化之前先验证证据

commonMisreads.en: A value proposition is not a slogan; it is a claim about specific jobs, pains, gains, and evidence of fit.
commonMisreads.zh: 价值主张不是口号；它是关于具体任务、痛点、收益和匹配证据的一组判断。

firstSteps.en: Read chapter 1 with the Value Proposition Canvas open.
firstSteps.zh: 打开价值主张画布同步阅读第 1 章。

firstSteps.en: Use chapter 3 before committing major product or channel investment.
firstSteps.zh: 在投入重大产品或渠道资源前阅读第 3 章。

outcomes.en: You can state which customer job matters most and what evidence would prove your solution creates value.
outcomes.zh: 你能说明哪个客户任务最重要，以及什么证据能证明你的方案创造了价值。

practicePrompts.en: Which customer job, pain, or gain in your current project is still an assumption rather than evidence?
practicePrompts.zh: 你当前项目里，哪一个客户任务、痛点或收益仍然只是猜测而不是证据？
